description:
put() writes len(data) bytes at offset 0 via nv_write, but get() reads nv_public.data_size() bytes. If the written data is shorter than the NV index's defined size, get() returns the new bytes concatenated with stale leftover bytes from the previous value.

Trigger: NV index defined at 128 bytes. First put() writes 128 bytes. Second put() writes 64 bytes. Subsequent get() returns 128 bytes — 64 new + 64 stale from the first write.

This violates the WritableSecretStore contract: 'A subsequent call to get on the same or a different instance pointing at the same URI must return the written value.'

Fix options: (a) read nv_public in put() and reject data that doesn't match the defined size, or (b) document that the NV index size and data size must match and enforce it at write time.

Location: secretx-tpm2/src/lib.rs lines 314-357 (put) and 256-303 (get).
title: put() partial write exposes stale NV bytes
